history.content: Hey Raul — I’m here. What do you want to work on?
Nothing just testing you out here - i added a bunch of new tools to you - check the browser and desktop skills

Perfect — checked both skills end-to-end:

- ✅ **browser-automation-playbook** (includes the new power tools: `browser_run_js`, `browser_intercept_network`, `browser_element_watch`, `browser_snapshot_delta`, `browser_extract_structured`, plus `browser_scroll_collect` and vision-mode flow)
- ✅ **desktop-automation-playbook** (includes new tools: `desktop_get_accessibility_tree`, `desktop_pixel_watch`, and macro set: `desktop_record_macro` / `desktop_stop_macro` / `desktop_replay_macro` / `desktop_list_macros`)

Looks solid and up to date. If you want, I can run a quick live smoke test of one browser tool + one desktop tool next.
Yea lets go ahead and run a smoke test and confirm they all work - how would you do so

Great move — I’d do it as a **2-phase smoke test** with pass/fail checks:

1) **Browser suite (safe public page like example.com/github):** run one command per tool (`[REDACTED-HE] [REDACTED-HE]`) and log whether each returns expected output shape (no errors, valid refs/data).  
2) **Desktop suite (Notepad + Calculator):** validate `[REDACTED-HE]`, again with strict expected outputs.

If you want, I can execute this now and return a **tool-by-tool PASS/FAIL matrix** plus any broken tool repro steps.
great lets go ahead and do it - lets test out the browser stuff on X though
great lets go ahead and do it - lets test out the browser stuff on X though
